sections.paragraph:
πετεινόν, οῦ, τό (πέτομαι) subst. neut. of πετεινός, ή, όν (Theognis, Trag., Hdt. et al.; LXX; TestLevi 9:13; Jos., Ant. 2, 245; 3, 137) any kind of bird (wild or domestic), bird, B 11:3 (Is 16:2). Mostly pl. (Hdt.; O. Wilck II, 1523 [127 b.c.]; LXX; En 7:5; PsSol 5:9; TestSol C prol. 3; TestJob 27:1; ParJer; Jos., Ant. 8, 40; SibOr 3, 224) Mt 13:4; Mk 4:4; Lk 12:24. W. κτήνη (Gen 8:17; Lev 7:26) Hs 9, 1, 8; 9, 24, 1. W. τετράποδα, ἑρπετά Ac 10:12; Ro 1:23. W. τετράποδα, θηρία, ἑρπετά (Herm. Wr. 1, 11b) Ac 11:6; w. still others PtK 2 p. 14, 17. W. θηρία, ἑρπετά, ἐνάλια Js 3:7. W. θηρία, ἰχθύες B 6:12, B 18 (s. ἑρπετόν on these combinations). τὰ π. τοῦ οὐρανοῦ (s. οὐρανός 1d) the birds in the sky=wild birds Mt 6:26; 8:20; 13:32 (cp. 1QH 8:9); Mk 4:32; Lk 8:5; 9:58; 13:19; Ac 10:12; 11:6; B 6:12 (Gen 1:28), 18; Hs 9, 24, 1; GJs 3:2; 18:2 codd. Of birds of prey B 10:10.—BHHW III 2111. DELG s.v. πέτομαι. M-M s.v. πετεινός.

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

Theognis Theognis , VI b.c.—List 5

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. pl. = plural
  2. Wilck O. Wilck = UWilcken, ed., Griechische Ostraka aus Ägypten u. Nubien—List 4

En En = I Enoch—List 2

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

prol. prol. = prologus (prolog, prologue)

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1QH 1QH = Psalms of Thanksgiving (Hôdāyôt)—List 2, end

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 809
